title: Audit-outcome reflection log feeding phase5-skeptic (firewall-safe TradingAgents memory loop)
source: Analysis of D:\PROJECTS\TradingAgents-main (Tauric Research multi-agent LLM trading framework) for transferable patterns, 2026-05-27. Most of it is LLM-as-trader (forbidden by our rule-based-primary contract + lookahead firewall), but three engineering patterns transfer.
why: TradingAgents' strongest idea is a two-phase outcome-memory loop (agents/utils/memory.py + graph/reflection.py): Phase A logs each decision as pending; Phase B fetches the realized return on the next run, generates a structured 2-4 sentence reflection (which thesis held/failed, one lesson), and injects recent lessons into future decision prompts. Loop A records tick outcomes to signals/loop_a_state.json but has NO structured cross-tick learning. The transferable, firewall-safe version: a durable audit-outcome reflection log — proposal survived/died, was the declared falsification criterion met, why — wired into the phase5-skeptic (end-of-day red-teamer) and human triage. CRITICAL: it must NOT feed the phase5-hypothesizer, which is firewalled from outcomes by design (.claude/skills/phase5-regime-methodology/SKILL.md). This gives the loop genuine memory of what auditing has and hasn't found, without reintroducing lookahead.
value_rationale: H: directly strengthens Loop A's self-correction (the autonomous-loop north-star) and addresses the skeptic's mandate to catch methodology drift across days — currently it has no persisted history of prior verdicts to compare against.
scope: Define a reflection-log schema (per audited proposal: id, regime, verdict, falsification criterion, met/unmet, realized audit metrics, 2-4 sentence lesson). Durable store under signals/ or results/loop_a/.
Phase A: at audit completion, append a pending reflection entry keyed by proposal id.
Phase B: resolve pending entries with the M3 audit outcome; generate the structured reflection.
Wire recent reflections into the phase5-skeptic context (end-of-day red-team), NOT the hypothesizer.
Firewall guard: add an explicit test/assertion that reflection text never reaches the hypothesizer's inputs (mirror the existing lookahead firewall checks).
recommendation: Build it skeptic-facing and human-facing first; resist any temptation to close the loop into the hypothesizer (that is the lookahead trap).
out_of_scope: Feeding outcomes to the hypothesizer (forbidden by firewall); importing TradingAgents' LLM debate-as-signal mechanism; any live-trading reflection.
